'''Scott Moore''' (born 23 January 1988) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|hk}}.

He played in the [[Super League]] for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[London Broncos]] and [[Castleford Tigers]], and in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Moore finished his playing career with [[Rochdale Hornets]] and [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Moore was the youngest ever Super League player when he made his debut for Saints at the [[JJB Stadium]] aged only 16.<ref>{{cite web |title=Coach defends Moore debut |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/st_helens/3595010.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=27 January 2026 |date=24 August 2004}}</ref>

Moore spent 2008 on loan at [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].
[[File:Scott Moore.JPG|thumb|right|Moore playing for Huddersfield]]

He spent 2009 on loan at Huddersfield whilst playing hooker. In 2009, Moore was named in the Super League team of the year at Hooker.

Moore returned to St Helens for the start of the 2010 season. Moore played in the [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=Guardian|accessdate=21 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=Daily Telegraph|accessdate=21 November 2019}}</ref>

On 11 October 2011, it was announced that Moore has signed a three-year deal commencing in 2012 with English Super League side [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]. On Friday 17 February 2012, Widnes Vikings announced they had suspended Moore, [[Hep Cahill]] and [[Simon Finnigan]] for an unspecified breach of club discipline, so missing the match against Salford City Reds on Sunday 19 February 2012.<ref name="Vikings suspend trio">{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=7201|title=Vikings suspend trio|publisher=catalansdragons.com|date=17 February 2012|accessdate=17 February 2012|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120513072629/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=7201|archivedate=13 May 2012|df=dmy-all}}</ref> On 12 April 2012, Moore was sacked by Widnes for a further breach of discipline.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/17693189|title = Widnes allow hooker Moore exit|work = BBC Sport}}</ref>

On 17 April 2012, Scott signed for Huddersfield until the end of the season.

In early November 2012 it was announced that Moore had signed a two-year-contract with the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].

In mid January 2014, the [[London Broncos]] announced they signed Moore for the 2014 season where he was their starting hooker.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/5147.php#.UtiILp9FDqA |title=News {{!}} London Broncos Rugby League {{!}} Official Site : Broncos Sign England Hooker |website=www.londonbroncosrl.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140118112336/http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/5147.php |archive-date=2014-01-18}}</ref>

Following the Broncos relegation from Super League he signed a deal with the Castleford from the 2015 season.

Moore was loaned to [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in 2015, with [[Paul McShane (rugby league)|Paul McShane]] going in the opposite direction.

He later signed a permanent deal for the 2016 season with [[Wakefield Trinity]].

Moore signed for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] on a four-game trial. This turned into a year full time deal. However at the end of the season Moore was released.

On 19 June 2019 it was reported that he had signed for [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] in the [[RFL Championship]] in a swap deal with [[Kyle Shelford]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 Jun 2019|accessdate=8 Sep 2021|title=Scott Moore leaves Rochdale for Swinton in swap deal|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/scott-moore-leaves-rochdale-for-swinton-in-swap-deal/}}</ref>

===International career===
Moore made his test début for England in 2009, playing two games.

==Personal life==
On 6 March 2018 Moore was jailed for 23 months at [[Bolton Crown Court]] for dangerous driving and assaulting three police officers.<ref>{{cite news |url=http://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/news/16066501.Rugby_League_player_jailed_after_assaulting_three_police_officers_following_high_speed_chase/ |title=Rugby League player Scott Moore jailed after assaulting three police officers following high-speed chase |newspaper=St Helens Star |date=6 March 2018 |accessdate=6 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-england-manchester-43306423|title=Rugby player Scott Moore jailed after high-speed chase and assaults on police|work=BBC News|date=6 March 2018}}</ref>
